title: HoloLand Frontier Encounter Manifest
description: Review schema for HoloLand frontier encounters. Apex-tier encounters must declare the player's mental model they attack before any content can be accepted.

playerAssumptionGate.description: For apex-tier encounters, reviewers must confirm cognitiveAttackVector.targetedAssumption was stated as one sentence.

targetedAssumption.description: One sentence naming the player's model-of-world assumption that the apex encounter targets. PRs without this sentence are rejected.
examples: Player assumes trusted NPC guidance is safe to follow.
Player assumes private voice chat cannot be perceived by the world.
Player assumes the only valid victory condition is reducing HP to zero.

playerSignal.description: Observable player behavior proving the assumption was engaged.

designSafeguard.description: How the encounter stays fair, replayable, and consent-respecting while attacking the assumption.

paper21ThreatModel.description: Optional link to the adversarial trust injection threat model category.

examples.title: Mercy Duel
examples.summary: A duel whose resolution depends on recognizing that mercy, not HP depletion, is the real victory condition.

mechanics.winCondition: Player chooses mercy after identifying the encounter's hidden social contract.
mechanics.failureCondition: Player keeps pursuing HP depletion after the opponent has yielded.
mechanics.primaryLoop: Observe behavior, test assumptions, and decide whether continuing combat is still valid.

cognitiveAttackVector.targetedAssumption: Player assumes the only valid victory condition is reducing HP to zero.
cognitiveAttackVector.playerSignal: Player stops attacking after the encounter changes its stance and dialogue.
cognitiveAttackVector.designSafeguard: The encounter gives visible state changes and repeatable dialogue before punishing a wrong assumption.

review.safetyReview: Uses original names and mechanics; no protected characters, lore, or setting material.
